steps.title: Overview: User Outputs
steps.description: The workbench tour showed how state changes. This tour shows what users get from that state: preview summaries, readiness checks, purchase lists, SVG export, 3MF export, static file links, sheet dialogs, and share URLs.

steps.title: Readiness Summary
steps.description: Build readiness condenses diagnostics into one export-facing message. It reports warnings when present and otherwise returns a ready state for the current layout.

steps.title: Export Diagnostics UI
steps.description: The workbench renders active diagnostics into the fabrication area. This code reads the active layout and shows the current warnings without mutating the model.

steps.title: Export Controls
steps.description: Export controls are synchronized from layout and fabrication mode. The button text, visible print design controls, purchase list, and design-specific controls all update together.

steps.title: Purchase List
steps.description: The purchase list derives required off-the-shelf parts from the active layout. Static references can provide their own source files and license links, while generated designs use chosen fan and filter presets.

steps.title: SVG Export
steps.description: SVG export creates a blob from the layout SVG and triggers a browser download. The drawing content already came from the layout and Boxes renderer.

steps.title: Print Export
steps.description: Print export branches between static reference file links and generated 3MF packages. Generated packages come from the print design export path, not the preview scene.

steps.title: Sheet Dialog
steps.description: The sheet dialog uses the same active print sheet plan as the main preview. Opening the dialog changes display scale, not the underlying part data.

steps.title: Preview Summary
steps.description: Preview summaries are design-aware. Static references, Corsi, donut, and generated Nukit designs each get the summary fields that make sense for that model.

steps.title: Quick Check
steps.text: Where does generated print export get its geometry?
answers: From `createPrintDesignThreeMfExport` and the printable kit path
From reading pixels out of the canvas
steps.explanation: `exportPrintKit` starts at line 1431 in `src/app/main.ts` and calls `createPrintDesignThreeMfExport` for generated print designs.

steps.title: Summary
steps.description: User outputs are downstream views of layout and kit data: diagnostics, summaries, downloads, purchase guidance, and print sheets. The final tour covers tests, scripts, config, and reference material that help keep those outputs stable.
